default_binding.LAND_GREED_TITLE: The Price of the Coin Tray

slots.meaning: The hero's ally, a boatman who ferries and guides them.
slots.guidance: A capable, loyal guide who waits at the rendezvous and never becomes a source of peril himself.

slots.meaning: The city whose canals the whole heist takes place in.
slots.guidance: A place name; the canal-laced backdrop of the run.

slots.meaning: The building being robbed (the whole bounded, explorable heist site).
slots.guidance: A single named building the hero infiltrates; wonder/tension over dread; no toxic air, no lethal architecture.

slots.meaning: The landmark where the hero starts out and meets the companion.
slots.guidance: A quiet, safe waypoint before the run begins.

slots.meaning: The landmark where the companion waits for pickup at the end of the run.
slots.guidance: A distinctive, memorable meeting-point landmark near the water.

slots.meaning: The target vault/strongroom holding the prize.
slots.guidance: A sealed, valuable room; safe to approach, a puzzle to open, not a death-trap.

slots.meaning: The distinctive lock mechanism securing the vault.
slots.guidance: A puzzle-lock, meant to be read/solved, not forced; no weapon imagery.

slots.meaning: The full descriptive name of the haul: the strongbox and its contents together.
slots.guidance: A short descriptive phrase for the prize; a benign, carriable treasure, not a weapon or graphic hazard.

slots.meaning: The incriminating document inside the vault (the true prize of the three endings).
slots.guidance: A record/ledger object; a benign paper prize.

slots.meaning: The short nickname used for the haul once it is in hand.
slots.guidance: A one-word slang term for 'the take'; benign, carriable.

slots.meaning: The mid-run escalation point after which the low routes become dangerous.
slots.guidance: A survivable escalation cue (a bell, a signal); must read as a fair-warning threshold, not an instant kill switch.

slots.meaning: The recurring countdown cue used throughout the whole run; the story's literal clock.
slots.guidance: A repeating, audible/visible signal (a bell, a chime); gives fair warning, escalates gradually.

slots.meaning: The bottleneck structure guarding entry to the counting-house grounds.
slots.guidance: A physical barrier/gate; passable by more than one method.

slots.meaning: The patrol/guard force watching the exposed routes at night.
slots.guidance: Human patrol figures; a threat of capture, not violence.

slots.meaning: The name of the first (fast, exposed) approach route.
slots.guidance: A distinct, nameable canal route; fast but watched.

slots.meaning: The name of the second (quiet, rising-water) approach route.
slots.guidance: A distinct, nameable route; quiet but filling with water.

slots.meaning: The name of the third (overland, always-watched) approach route.
slots.guidance: A distinct, nameable route; a trap, always watched.

slots.meaning: The rusted chain mechanism on the water-gate.
slots.guidance: A mechanical fixture; noisy if forced.

slots.meaning: The tangled net obstacle underwater at the gate.
slots.guidance: A snag hazard; frees with effort, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The small door's stubborn latch mechanism.
slots.guidance: A fiddly, pickable latch.

slots.meaning: The silent guard creature patrolling the yard.
slots.guidance: A watchful animal on a chain; a capture/noise risk, not a weapon.

slots.meaning: The service hatch offering a way into the cellars.
slots.guidance: A grimy service entrance.

slots.meaning: The night-shift clerk on duty near the stair.
slots.guidance: A human obstacle; capture risk if noticed, not violence.

slots.meaning: The watchman patrolling the counting-floor with a lantern.
slots.guidance: A human patrol figure; capture risk, not violence.

slots.meaning: The tall stacks of paper offering cover along the wall.
slots.guidance: A tall, precarious stack; noisy if it falls, not dangerous.

slots.meaning: The raised interior walkway/hatch offering a long way to the vault.
slots.guidance: An elevated structural feature.

slots.meaning: The fast-running water channel that floods on the escape route.
slots.guidance: A fast channel; dangerous if entered at the wrong time, described in the fixed prose, not the slot value.

slots.meaning: The exposed rooftop escape path.
slots.guidance: A high, exposed path.

slots.meaning: The slow, hidden channel through the reeds near the junction.
slots.guidance: A quiet, concealing channel.

slots.meaning: The watch-post overlooking the final open basin.
slots.guidance: A guard post with a clear sightline.

slots.meaning: The tray of counted coin left out near the vault landing.
slots.guidance: A tempting, noisy side-prize distinct from the true haul.

slots.meaning: Ending title: the full, clean success (whole haul carried out).
slots.guidance: A short, triumphant title. Names ending e_clean.

slots.meaning: Ending title: the partial success (ledger kept, coin sacrificed).
slots.guidance: A short, bittersweet title. Names ending e_cost.

slots.meaning: Ending title: walking away empty-handed but alive and free.
slots.guidance: A short, wry title about survival over the take. Names ending e_walkaway.

slots.meaning: Ending title: caught crossing the open basin (fixed CAPTURE ending).
slots.guidance: Names a fixed capture ending; keep it evocative and age-appropriate. This slot legitimately NAMES a capture outcome, so 'capture' is not declared in its own forbid list; it still may not describe a weapon or graphic harm.

slots.meaning: Ending title: swept away in the flooding side channel (fixed DEATH ending).
slots.guidance: Names a fixed death ending. Per the band-exception rule (WS-2 design sec. 14, 8.3), this slot must NOT forbid 'lethal': the title is permitted to read as fatal, since it names an already-fixed death outcome rather than describing a retreatable hazard. It still must not contain a weapon or graphic/gory imagery.

slots.meaning: Ending title: caught behind the night-clerk on the stair (fixed CAPTURE ending).
slots.guidance: Names a fixed capture ending; keep it evocative, no weapon/graphic imagery.

slots.meaning: Ending title: caught for the sake of a handful of coin (fixed CAPTURE ending).
slots.guidance: Names a fixed capture ending; keep it evocative, no weapon/graphic imagery.
